ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for salted-egg-coated tater tots; mapped to broader potato product class. Salted egg yolk coating is a Singaporean fusion preparation not yet represented in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Potato products and dishes) is the closest defensible parent node. No specific code exists for salted-egg-coated deep-fried potato tots in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fusion snack preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Salted Egg Tater Tots is approximately 100 g (6–8 pieces), commonly served as a snack or side dish in Singaporean casual dining and hawker-style restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Salted Egg Tater Tots — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient record G00010398
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) taken directly from HPB Foodo record G00010398. Secondary nutrients (sodium, saturated fat, dietary fibre, sugar) estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for deep-fried potato products and salted duck egg yolk, as HPB record does not publish full micronutrient breakdown. See notes for detail.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for salted-egg-coated tater tots. GI estimated at 75 (high) based on deep-fried potato products (GI ~70–80) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The salted egg yolk coating adds fat and protein which may marginally lower GI, but the dominant carbohydrate matrix is deep-fried potato starch, supporting a high-GI classification. GL per 100 g serving = (75 × 2.36) / 100 = 1.77, rounded to 1.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium from salted duck egg yolk (~650 mg/100 g), exceeding the 600 mg/100 g threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Salted Egg Tater Tots due to the very high sodium content (~650 mg/100 g) contributed by salted duck egg yolk, which substantially raises blood pressure; avoid pairing with additional high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI deep-fried potato base raises postprandial blood glucose; salted egg yolk adds saturated fat worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Salted Egg Tater Tots because the deep-fried potato base has a high glycaemic index (~75), causing rapid postprandial glucose spikes, and the salted egg yolk coating contributes saturated fat that worsens insulin resistance over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying generates trans fats and AGEs; salted egg yolk adds cholesterol, saturated fat, and very high sodium — all adverse for cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Salted Egg Tater Tots as the combination of deep-fried potato (generating oxidised lipids and AGEs), salted egg yolk (high in cholesterol and saturated fat), and very high sodium (~650 mg/100 g) collectively raises LDL cholesterol, promotes arterial stiffness, and elevates blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs worsening joint inflammation; potato is a nightshade that may trigger symptoms; very high sodium worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Salted Egg Tater Tots as deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that exacerbate joint inflammation, potato is a nightshade vegetable that may trigger flares in sensitive patients, and the very high sodium from salted egg yolk worsens RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salted Egg Tater Tots are a popular Singaporean fusion snack consisting of bite-sized deep-fried potato tots coated in a rich, savoury salted duck egg yolk sauce. The dish is a modern hawker-inspired creation that combines the Western tater tot format with the distinctly Southeast Asian flavour profile of salted egg yolk, which is made from duck eggs cured in brine or salt paste. The salted egg yolk sauce is typically prepared by frying the yolk with butter, curry leaves, chilli, and sugar to create a foamy, intensely savoury-umami coating that clings to each crispy potato tot. The texture contrast between the crunchy deep-fried potato exterior and the creamy, slightly grainy salted egg yolk coating is a defining characteristic. Nutritionally, the HPB Singapore Foodo record (G00010398) reports 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0 g fat, and 2.36 g carbohydrate per 100 g — an unusual macro profile for a deep-fried snack, likely reflecting the high protein contribution of the egg yolk component relative to the small potato portion. Sodium is estimated at approximately 650 mg/100 g due to the salted egg yolk, making this a high-sodium food. Clinically, this dish is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high-GI potato base, saturated fat from egg yolk), cardiovascular disease (cholesterol, saturated fat, sodium, AGEs from deep-frying), and rheumatoid arthritis (AGEs, nightshade ingredient, high sodium). It is commonly served as a snack or side dish in Singaporean casual dining, zi char restaurants, and hawker stalls.
common_user_queries: Is salted egg tater tots healthy?
Can diabetics eat salted egg tater tots?
Salted egg tater tots sodium content

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record G00010398 (exact match, high confidence for those four values). However, the reported macro profile (0 g fat per 100 g for a deep-fried salted-egg-coated snack) is nutritionally anomalous and may reflect a data entry issue or a specific product formulation; this warrants caution. Secondary nutrients (sodium, saturated fat, dietary fibre, sugar) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries and the health_suitability reasoning in the input record. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is medium.
notes: ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): fat_g is reported as 0.0 per the HPB Foodo source record G00010398 — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo G00010398. This is nutritionally anomalous for a deep-fried salted-egg-coated snack and is flagged as a potential data quality issue in the source. The value is preserved as reported but should be reviewed against the original product label or a laboratory analysis. saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with the source-reported fat_g of 0.0; this is also flagged as anomalous. Contains no fat per source record G00010398; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific Singaporean fusion deep-fried snack are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010398 or in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. SODIUM ESTIMATE: sodium_mg of 650 mg/100 g is an estimate derived from the health_suitability reasoning in the input record and is consistent with known sodium levels in salted duck egg yolk preparations; this should be confirmed against a product label or laboratory analysis. ONTOLOGY: No exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for salted-egg-coated tater tots; broader parent codes used with gap notes. GI: Estimated from analogous deep-fried potato product GI values (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct measurement available for this specific product. ALIASES Q8 FIX (iteration 3): Hindi alias 'नमकीन अंडा टेटर टॉटस' added to both aliases_by_language.hindi and flat aliases array to ensure full consistency between structured and flat alias surfaces.
